raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assembly Bill 1264 will increase access to birth control by allowing Californians to be prescribed birth control via a smartphone app with fewer barriers.",
    "The current telehealth law requires patients to answer a health questionnaire, self-report their blood pressure, and schedule a video chat before submitting a request for contraceptives.",
    "Doctors find that a teleconference visit to access birth control is medically unnecessary.",
    "It is estimated that 50% of patients do not fulfill the current specifications to receive contraception.",
    "Assembly Bill 1264 clarifies the ability for birth control to be prescribed via teleconference without a visit or video chat.",
    "Unintended pregnancies account for nearly half of all pregnancies.",
    "Assembly Bill 1264 reduces barriers to reproductive telehealth services."
  ]
}
```
